title: Agrostis fasciculata
intro: Agrostis fasciculata, also known as tufted hairgrass, is a perennial grass species native to North America. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms associated with respiratory issues and improve overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Agrostis fasciculata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential uses in modern medicine.
taxonomy: Agrostis fasciculata is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is a member of the genus Agrostis, which includes several species of bentgrasses. Within the Poaceae family, Agrostis fasciculata is part of the subfamily Pooideae. This classification places Agrostis fasciculata among other cool-season grasses. Further classification includes the tribe Poeae and the subtribe Poinae.

It has been traditionally used in some cultures to treat wounds and skin conditions. More studies are required to fully understand the medicinal potential of Agrostis fasciculata.
